name: Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular via the Camino Frances
name_original: Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular por el Camino Frances
region_intro: Hiking around Obanos is characterized by its position on the historic Camino de Santiago, where the Aragonese and Navarrese Ways converge. The landscape features rolling hills, expansive fields, and vineyards, particularly within the Valdizarbe valley. Trails often pass through medieval villages and offer views from elevated points like the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Arnotegui. This region provides a blend of cultural heritage and diverse rural scenery for hikers. Best hiking trails around Obanos The most popular hiking route is Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular via the Camino Frances, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This route leads to the historic Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Muruzabal-Arnotegi – circular via the Camino Frances, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail passes by the Hermitage of Arnotegui, offering panoramic views of the Valdizarbe valley. Local hikers also love the View of Cirauqui – Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge loop from Muruzábal, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with expansive views, often completed in about 5 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Obanos is defined by rolling hills, expansive fields, and historic Camino de Santiago paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Obanos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Obanos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Obanos?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes in the Obanos area, offering a diverse range of experiences. The komoot community has recorded 69 tours, with options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there hiking routes suitable for beginners in Obanos?
A: Yes, Obanos offers several easy hiking routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968926711/obanos-puente-la-reina-circular-via-the-camino-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular via the Camino Frances&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.6 km trail that allows you to experience a section of the historic Camino.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Obanos?
A: Hiking around Obanos takes you through picturesque landscapes characterized by rolling hills, expansive fields, and vineyards, especially within the Valdizarbe valley. You'll often pass through charming medieval villages and enjoy views from elevated points.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance hikes around Obanos?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are several longer and more demanding routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cirauqui – Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge loop from Muruzábal&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.8 km trail with significant elevation changes, requiring good fitness.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover on hikes around Obanos?
A: Many trails in the area are steeped in history. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/119191" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century structure built for pilgrims. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint John the Baptist&lt;/a&gt; in Obanos itself, and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de Eunate&lt;/a&gt; are also significant historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any trails with panoramic viewpoints in the Obanos area?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968917040/muruzabal-arnotegi-circular-via-the-camino-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muruzabal-Arnotegi – circular via the Camino Frances&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the Hermitage of Arnotegui, which offers privileged panoramic views of the Valdizarbe valley and Las Nequeas. Many routes also pass through or offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, a high point with expansive vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Obanos?
A: The trails around Obanos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of cultural heritage with diverse rural scenery, the well-maintained paths of the Camino de Santiago, and the charming medieval villages along the way.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Obanos?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Obanos are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968926711/obanos-puente-la-reina-circular-via-the-camino-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular via the Camino Frances&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular easy option.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails around Obanos using public transport?
A: Obanos is well-connected, and some trails are accessible by public transport. The village's central location on the Camino de Santiago means it's a known point for travelers, and local bus services can connect you to nearby towns and trailheads. It's advisable to check local schedules for specific routes.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Obanos suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easier and shorter routes, particularly those that are circular and have minimal elevation gain, are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968926711/obanos-puente-la-reina-circular-via-the-camino-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obanos-Puente la Reina – circular via the Camino Frances&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for a family-friendly outing.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Obanos?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Obanos, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn showcases vibrant foliage. Summer can be hot, especially in the open fields, and winter can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round.

Q: How does the Camino de Santiago influence hiking in Obanos?
A: Obanos is a crucial junction on the Camino de Santiago, where the Aragonese and Navarrese Ways converge. This means many hiking trails are well-trodden pilgrim paths, offering clear signage and a unique historical and cultural atmosphere. You'll share the trails with pilgrims from around the world, adding to the experience.
tips: This place of worship, which is named after the Holy Cross (Latin "Santa Croce"), relates its patronage to the cross on which Jesus Christ died. The veneration of the Holy Cross goes back in large part to Saint Helena, who was the mother of Emperor Constantine the Great (who, after his victory at the "Milvian Bridge" outside Rome, recognized Christianity as a religion and in 312 the long time of persecution ended with the Milan “Tolerance Edict”). In the early fourth century she undertook a pilgrimage to the Holy Land (Israel / Palestine) and brought numerous relics from there to Europe, including the "True Cross" on which Jesus Christ was crucified. Helena is therefore often depicted with a cross as an attribute. The feast of the discovery, public presentation and veneration of the Holy Cross - called "Exaltation of the Cross" - is celebrated by the Church on September 14th.

With an unmistakable Gothic style on the outside, its construction dates back to the 16th century, which preserves some vestiges of the old Romanesque temple on which it was built. It is the most important church in the town of Puente la Reina and a place of great interest for all the pilgrims who travel the Camino de Santiago Frances.

Puente la Reina (Basque Gares) is a municipality with 2840 inhabitants. In Puente la Reina, the Aragonese and Navarrese branches of the Way of St. James meet, the common path crossing the Arga river via the bridge of the same name.

The name and development are linked to the bridge donated by Dona Mayor, Queen of Navarre, wife of King Sancho Mayor, or her daughter-in-law. Despite all the uncertainty regarding the founder, the construction of the bridge in the first half of the 11th century is certain.

Because river crossings were dangerous, detours were long and ferry services expensive, the streams of pilgrims soon concentrated on this bridge. This was followed by the settlement of Franks and the founding of a market town. The development of the city is an example of the infrastructure projects of this time and their effect, and can often be found in a comparable form on the Way of St. James.

In 1122 it received city rights, from 1235 the construction of the city wall began. García IV donated the city to the Templars, and after their dissolution at the beginning of the 14th century, it passed to the Hospitallers.
The church of San Juan Bautista was built at the beginning of the 20th century and is in the neo-Gothic style. It is located in the beautiful Plaza de los Fueros, in Obanos.

Church of Saint John the Baptist, Obanos
